---
title: "Cost of service by heating subclass (RIE)"
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook builds **two pairs** of tables (totals + per-customer averages): delivery revenue, cost of service (marginal + EPMC residual), and cross-subsidy by `postprocess_group.heating_type_v2`, for the rate-case test-year batch.

**Data source:** After CAIRO runs finish, post-processing writes **master tables** under `s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates/<state>/all_utilities/<batch>/run_<delivery>+<supply>/`. We read the **master BAT** dataset (`cross_subsidization_BAT_values`), which already decomposes each building’s annual electric bill into delivery vs supply and carries BAT metrics plus marginal-cost and residual components.

- **Run 1+2** — status quo: **uniform default** delivery+supply for all customers (same upgrade-0 stock as `analysis.qmd`).
- **Run 17+18** — **HP customers on the HP flat tariff; everyone else on the non-HP (recalibrated default) tariff** (same building stock as 1+2). See `analysis.qmd` (~3776–3778).

## Setup

First we import the libraries we use end-to-end: Polars for the S3 parquet work, Great Tables for the formatted table, and `Path` for writing cache files. We also start an empty `report_vars` dict so any statistics we want to reuse from the narrative can be pickled later.

```{python}
#| label: setup

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import polars as pl
from great_tables import GT, loc, style

report_vars: dict = {}
```

## Parameters

Next we pin the **utility**, **batch name**, and **S3 prefixes** to master BAT for **run 1+2** (default) and **run 17+18** (HP flat / non-HP split), matching `PATH_MASTER_BAT_12` and `PATH_MASTER_BAT_1718` in `analysis.qmd`. Customer weights are already scaled in `run_scenario`; we still load **test-year customer count** and **total delivery revenue requirement** from the revenue-requirement YAML so sanity checks match the simulation config. `fetch_rdp_file` pulls that YAML at a fixed ref (update `RDP_REF` when you bump config). We define the canonical order and labels for the five `heating_type_v2` buckets.

```{python}
#| label: params

import yaml

from lib.rdp import fetch_rdp_file, parse_urdb_json

UTILITY = "rie"
BATCH = "ri_20260331_r1-20_rate_case_test_year"
STATE_LOWER = "ri"
S3_BASE = "s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates"
PATH_MASTER_BAT_12 = (
    f"{S3_BASE}/{STATE_LOWER}/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_1+2/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"
)
PATH_MASTER_BAT_1718 = (
    f"{S3_BASE}/{STATE_LOWER}/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_17+18/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"
)

# rate-design-platform rev for revenue-requirement YAML (align with analysis.qmd when updating)
RDP_REF = "e9e5088"
_PATH_RIE_RATE_CASE_YAML = (
    "rate_design/hp_rates/ri/config/rev_requirement/rie_rate_case_test_year.yaml"
)
_RAW_REV_YAML = fetch_rdp_file(_PATH_RIE_RATE_CASE_YAML, RDP_REF)
_rev_yaml = yaml.safe_load(_RAW_REV_YAML)
TEST_YEAR_CUSTOMER_COUNT = float(_rev_yaml["test_year_customer_count"])
TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT = float(_rev_yaml["total_delivery_revenue_requirement"])
# Integer customer total for table display (419,348 for current YAML); subclasses scale to this.
DISPLAY_CUSTOMER_TOTAL = int(round(TEST_YEAR_CUSTOMER_COUNT))

# Testimony / narrative: PRB-1 residential delivery totals from the same YAML (pickled with report_vars).
_drr0 = _rev_yaml["delivery_revenue_requirement"]
report_vars["rie_rev_req_total_delivery_rr"] = TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT
report_vars["rie_rev_req_test_year_customer_count"] = TEST_YEAR_CUSTOMER_COUNT
report_vars["rie_rev_req_customer_charge_total"] = float(_drr0["customer_charge"]["total_budget"])
report_vars["rie_rev_req_core_delivery_total"] = float(_drr0["core_delivery_rate"]["total_budget"])
report_vars["rie_rev_req_test_year_residential_kwh"] = _ty_kwh = float(
    _rev_yaml["test_year_residential_kwh"],
)
report_vars["rie_rev_req_core_delivery_usd_per_kwh"] = (
    report_vars["rie_rev_req_core_delivery_total"] / _ty_kwh
)

# Testimony: transmission, renewable roll-up, EE programs charge, then remaining delivery top-ups (matches COS stacked bar).
_dtu0 = _rev_yaml["delivery_top_ups"]
_trans_budget = float(_dtu0["transmission_charge"]["total_budget"]) + float(
    _dtu0["transmission_uncollectible"]["total_budget"],
)
report_vars["rie_rev_req_transmission_total"] = _trans_budget
report_vars["rie_rev_req_transmission_usd_per_kwh"] = _trans_budget / _ty_kwh
_renewable_energy_keys_ty = (
    "long_term_contracting_charge",
    "net_metering_charge",
    "renewables_charge",
    "re_growth_charge",
)
report_vars["rie_rev_req_renewable_energy_total"] = sum(
    float(_dtu0[k]["total_budget"])
    for k in _renewable_energy_keys_ty
    if k in _dtu0 and isinstance(_dtu0[k], dict) and "total_budget" in _dtu0[k]
)
_ee_prog_key = "energy_efficiency_programs_charge"
report_vars["rie_rev_req_energy_efficiency_total"] = float(
    _dtu0[_ee_prog_key]["total_budget"],
)
_other_fixed = 0.0
_other_vol = 0.0
for _pk, _row in _dtu0.items():
    if _pk in ("transmission_charge", "transmission_uncollectible"):
        continue
    if _pk in _renewable_energy_keys_ty:
        continue
    if _pk == _ee_prog_key:
        continue
    if not isinstance(_row, dict) or "total_budget" not in _row:
        continue
    _tb = float(_row["total_budget"])
    _cu = str(_row.get("charge_unit", "")).lower()
    if "/month" in _cu:
        _other_fixed += _tb
    else:
        _other_vol += _tb
_other_total = _other_fixed + _other_vol
report_vars["rie_rev_req_other_delivery_total"] = _other_total
report_vars["rie_rev_req_other_delivery_usd_per_kwh"] = _other_vol / _ty_kwh
report_vars["rie_rev_req_other_delivery_blended_c_per_kwh"] = (
    100.0 * _other_vol / _ty_kwh
)
_n_ty = float(TEST_YEAR_CUSTOMER_COUNT)
report_vars["rie_rev_req_other_delivery_avg_fixed_usd_per_mo"] = (
    _other_fixed / 12.0 / _n_ty if _n_ty > 0 else float("nan")
)

_RDP_RIE_ELEC_TARIFF_DIR = "rate_design/hp_rates/ri/config/tariffs/electric"


def _rie_calibrated_volumetric_usd_per_kwh(*, rel_filename: str) -> float:
    """First energy tier in URDB-shaped delivery JSON → `$/kWh` (matches CAIRO calibrated tariff input)."""
    path = f"{_RDP_RIE_ELEC_TARIFF_DIR}/{rel_filename}"
    doc = parse_urdb_json(fetch_rdp_file(path, RDP_REF))
    return float(doc["items"][0]["energyratestructure"][0][0]["rate"])


def _rie_calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(*, rel_filename: str) -> str:
    """First energy tier in URDB-shaped delivery JSON → ¢/kWh for table copy (matches CAIRO input)."""
    usd_kwh = _rie_calibrated_volumetric_usd_per_kwh(rel_filename=rel_filename)
    return f"{usd_kwh * 100:.2f}¢/kWh"


_C_KWH_TODAY_UNIFORM = _rie_calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(rel_filename="rie_default_calibrated.json")
_C_KWH_PROP_HP = _rie_calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(rel_filename="rie_hp_flat_calibrated.json")
_C_KWH_PROP_OTHER = _rie_calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(rel_filename="rie_nonhp_default_calibrated.json")

_default_vol_usd_kwh = _rie_calibrated_volumetric_usd_per_kwh(rel_filename="rie_default_calibrated.json")
_hp_flat_vol_usd_kwh = _rie_calibrated_volumetric_usd_per_kwh(rel_filename="rie_hp_flat_calibrated.json")
report_vars["hp_flat_volumetric_delivery_pct_below_default"] = (
    (_default_vol_usd_kwh - _hp_flat_vol_usd_kwh) / _default_vol_usd_kwh
    if _default_vol_usd_kwh > 0
    else float("nan")
)

# Annual fixed delivery charges per customer (customer charge + fixed delivery top-ups).
_annual_fixed_per_customer = (
    TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT - _default_vol_usd_kwh * _ty_kwh
) / TEST_YEAR_CUSTOMER_COUNT

# PRB-1 volumetric components (¢/kWh): core delivery vs transmission + uncollectible, from YAML budgets ÷ test-year kWh.
_drr = _rev_yaml["delivery_revenue_requirement"]
_dtu = _rev_yaml["delivery_top_ups"]
_C_KWH_DIST_FILING = f"{100.0 * float(_drr['core_delivery_rate']['total_budget']) / _ty_kwh:.2f}¢/kWh"
_C_KWH_TRANS_FILING = (
    f"{100.0 * (float(_dtu['transmission_charge']['total_budget']) + float(_dtu['transmission_uncollectible']['total_budget'])) / _ty_kwh:.2f}¢/kWh"
)

_COS_SUBTITLE_VOL_DEFAULT = (
    f"Total delivery rate {_C_KWH_TODAY_UNIFORM}, out of which distribution was {_C_KWH_DIST_FILING} "
    f"and transmission was {_C_KWH_TRANS_FILING}."
)

_COS_HP_COMPARE_SPAN_TODAY = (
    "All customers 2025 default rates " +
    f"({_C_KWH_TODAY_UNIFORM})"
)
_COS_HP_COMPARE_SPAN_PROPOSED = (
    f"HP customers on proposed rates ({_C_KWH_PROP_HP}); all others on adjusted default rates ({_C_KWH_PROP_OTHER})"
)
_COS_HP_COMPARE_SPAN_COS = "Cost of service and cross-subsidy under the proposal"

_COS_HP_AVG_COMPARE_SPAN_DEFAULT = (
    "All customers on 2025 default rates " +
    f"({_C_KWH_TODAY_UNIFORM})"
)
_COS_HP_AVG_COMPARE_SPAN_PROPOSED = (
    "Change in average delivery bill, then per-customer averages under proposed split rates — "
    + _COS_HP_COMPARE_SPAN_PROPOSED
)

HT_V2_ORDER = (
    "heat_pump",
    "electrical_resistance",
    "natgas",
    "delivered_fuels",
    "other",
)

HT_V2_LABELS: dict[str, str] = {
    "heat_pump": "Heat pump",
    "electrical_resistance": "Electric resistance",
    "natgas": "Natural gas",
    "delivered_fuels": "Delivered fuels",
    "other": "Other",
}
```

## RIE revenue requirement line items (PRB-1 / PRB-4 YAML)

The rate-design-platform file `rie_rate_case_test_year.yaml` splits **delivery** revenue into the **rate-case** components (`delivery_revenue_requirement`) plus **delivery top-ups** (`delivery_top_ups`). YAML lines are **rolled up** into six **areas** for display: volumetric renewables and growth program → **Renewable Energy**; O&M, capex, storm fund, arrearage, low-income discounts, system reliability, LIHEAP, EE/SRP uncollectible, transition, pension adjustment, and performance incentive → **Other Delivery Charge**. The **program line-item table** lists every delivery line in the YAML, including **credits** (negative `total_budget`) and the **performance incentive** line (`total_budget` = 0). The **stacked bar** still uses only **positive** budgets and omits the performance incentive, so segment widths sum to 100% of those amounts. Where a rolled-up chart category combines multiple lines, its aggregated **charge unit** and **rate comment** show an em dash. In-bar rate lines use **blended ¢/kWh** for pooled volumetric budgets, **`$`/month from the YAML comment** (or `total_budget ÷ customer_count ÷ 12` when `customer_count` is on the line) for fixed charges — not aggregate utility `$/mo`.

```{python}
#| label: rie-rev-req-budget-dataset

import re

from lib.plotnine import SB_COLORS

_RIE_RR_BUCKETS = (
    "delivery_revenue_requirement",
    "delivery_top_ups",
)
_RIE_RR_BUCKET_LABELS = {
    "delivery_revenue_requirement": "Delivery (PRB-1 rate case)",
    "delivery_top_ups": "Delivery top-ups",
}


def build_rie_rate_case_budget_line_items(
    yd: dict,
    raw_yaml: str,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    rows: list[dict] = []
    for bucket in _RIE_RR_BUCKETS:
        sub = yd.get(bucket) or {}
        if not isinstance(sub, dict):
            continue
        for pk, v in sub.items():
            if not isinstance(v, dict) or "total_budget" not in v:
                continue
            m = re.search(
                rf"(?m)^\s+{re.escape(pk)}:\s*\n(?:^[ \t].*\n)*?"
                rf"^[ \t]+charge_unit:\s*[^\n#]+(?:#\s*(.+))?\s*$",
                raw_yaml,
            )
            rate_comment = (m.group(1) or "").strip() if m else ""
            _cc_raw = v.get("customer_count")
            rows.append(
                {
                    "bucket": bucket,
                    "bucket_label": _RIE_RR_BUCKET_LABELS[bucket],
                    "program_key": pk,
                    "program_label": pk.replace("_", " ").title(),
                    "charge_unit": str(v.get("charge_unit", "")),
                    "rate_comment": rate_comment,
                    "total_budget": float(v["total_budget"]),
                    "customer_count": float(_cc_raw) if _cc_raw is not None else None,
                },
            )
    df = pl.DataFrame(rows)
    return df.with_columns(
        pl.when(
            (pl.col("rate_comment") == "")
            & (pl.col("program_key") == "supply_commodity_bundled"),
        )
        .then(pl.lit("Weighted bundled supply (monthly rates × scaled kWh)"))
        .otherwise(pl.col("rate_comment"))
        .alias("rate_comment"),
    )


def _rie_block_child_comments(lines: list[str], start_i: int) -> tuple[str | None, str | None, int]:
    """Scan indented child lines of `  program_key:` until next sibling program or interstitial source header."""
    cu_c = tb_c = None
    j = start_i + 1
    n = len(lines)
    while j < n:
        l2 = lines[j]
        if l2.startswith("  ") and not l2.startswith("    "):
            st2 = l2.strip()
            if re.match(r"^  [a-z_][a-z0-9_]*:\s*$", l2):
                break
            if st2.startswith("# --- From ") or st2.startswith("# https://www.documentcloud.org"):
                break
        if re.match(r"^[a-z_][a-z0-9_]*:\s*$", l2) and not l2.startswith(" "):
            break
        if "charge_unit:" in l2:
            m = re.search(r"#\s*(.+?)\s*$", l2)
            if m:
                cu_c = m.group(1).strip()
        if "total_budget:" in l2:
            m = re.search(r"#\s*(.+?)\s*$", l2)
            if m:
                tb_c = m.group(1).strip()
        j += 1
    return cu_c, tb_c, j


def _rie_parse_delivery_program_sources(raw: str) -> dict[str, dict[str, str | None]]:
    """Map each `delivery_revenue_requirement` / `delivery_top_ups` program_key → DocumentCloud cite, URL, YAML # notes."""
    lines = raw.splitlines()
    out: dict[str, dict[str, str | None]] = {}
    ctx: str | None = None
    cite: str | None = None
    url: str | None = None
    i = 0
    n = len(lines)
    while i < n:
        s = lines[i].rstrip("\n")
        st = s.strip()
        if st.startswith("# --- From "):
            inner = re.sub(r"^#\s*---\s*From\s+", "", st)
            inner = re.sub(r"\s*---\s*$", "", inner).strip()
            cite = f"RIE Book 21, {inner}"
            url = None
        elif st.startswith("# https://www.documentcloud.org"):
            url = "https://" + st[2:].split("https://", 1)[1].split()[0]
        elif re.match(r"^delivery_revenue_requirement:\s*$", s):
            ctx = "dr"
        elif re.match(r"^delivery_top_ups:\s*$", s):
            ctx = "dt"
        elif re.match(r"^supply_top_ups:\s*$", s):
            ctx = None
        elif ctx and re.match(r"^  ([a-z_][a-z0-9_]*):\s*$", s):
            pk = re.match(r"^  ([a-z_][a-z0-9_]*):\s*$", s).group(1)
            cu_c, tb_c, j = _rie_block_child_comments(lines, i)
            out[pk] = {
                "cite": cite,
                "url": url,
                "rate_formula": cu_c,
                "budget_formula": tb_c,
            }
            i = j - 1
        i += 1
    return out


def _rie_shorten_documentcloud_cite(cite: str) -> str:
    """Drop line/column locators from exhibit cites; keep page and exhibit id."""
    s = cite.strip()
    s = re.sub(r",?\s*lines?\s+\d+(?:\s*[–-]\s*\d+)?", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r",?\s*columns?\s+[^,]+", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+,", ",", s)
    s = re.sub(r",\s*,", ",", s)
    return s.strip().strip(",").strip()


def _rie_budget_derivation_is_trivial_sum(bf: str) -> bool:
    """True if `#` budget note is only numeric sums (no names, no multipliers)."""
    s = bf.strip()
    if not s or re.search(r"[a-zA-Z_]", s):
        return False
    if re.search(r"[×*x]", s, flags=re.I):
        return False
    return bool(re.fullmatch(r"[\d\s,+\-()]+", s))


def _rie_rate_note_redundant_vs_display(rate_display: str, rf: str) -> bool:
    """True when YAML rate note duplicates what the Rate column already shows."""
    rd = (rate_display or "").strip()
    rf = (rf or "").strip()
    if not rd or not rf or rd == "—":
        return False
    if rd.casefold() == rf.casefold():
        return True
    m_disp = re.search(r"(-?)([\d.]+)\s*¢/kwh", rd, re.I)
    m_file = re.search(r"(\(?)\s*\$?\s*([\d.]+)\s*/\s*kwh", rf, re.I)
    if m_disp and m_file:
        sign_d = -1.0 if m_disp.group(1) else 1.0
        c_disp = sign_d * float(m_disp.group(2))
        neg_usd = bool(m_file.group(1))
        usd = float(m_file.group(2))
        if neg_usd:
            usd = -abs(usd)
        c_file = 100.0 * usd
        return abs(c_disp - c_file) < 0.02
    mo_d = re.search(r"\$\s*([\d.]+)/mo", rd)
    mo_r = re.search(
        r"\$\s*([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*/\s*month",
        rf,
        flags=re.I,
    )
    if mo_d and mo_r:
        return (
            abs(
                float(mo_d.group(1))
                - float(mo_r.group(1).replace(",", "")),
            )
            < 0.02
        )
    return False


def _rie_format_sources_md(
    info: dict[str, str | None],
    *,
    rate_display: str | None = None,
) -> str:
    """Markdown for GT `fmt_markdown`: DocumentCloud links + YAML `#` notes when not redundant."""
    cite = _rie_shorten_documentcloud_cite(info.get("cite") or "")
    url = (info.get("url") or "").strip()
    rf = info.get("rate_formula")
    bf = info.get("budget_formula")
    rd = (rate_display or "").strip()
    parts: list[str] = []
    if cite and url:
        parts.append(f"**Rate:** [{cite}]({url})")
        parts.append(f"**Revenue:** [{cite}]({url})")
    elif cite:
        parts.append(f"**Rate & revenue:** {cite}")
    if rf and not _rie_rate_note_redundant_vs_display(rd, str(rf)):
        parts.append(f"**Rate (filing / note):** `{rf}`")
    if bf:
        bf_s = str(bf).strip()
        rf_s = str(rf or "").strip()
        if (
            not _rie_budget_derivation_is_trivial_sum(bf_s)
            and bf_s != rf_s
        ):
            parts.append(f"**Revenue (derivation):** `{bf_s}`")
    if not parts:
        return "—"
    return "  \n".join(parts)


rie_rr_total = float(_rev_yaml["total_delivery_revenue_requirement"])
_built_rr = build_rie_rate_case_budget_line_items(_rev_yaml, _RAW_REV_YAML)
_other_delivery_keys = (
    "o_m_charge",
    "capex_revenue",
    "storm_fund_replenishment_factor",
    "arrears_arrearage_recovery",
    "low_income_discounts",
    "system_reliability_plan_charge",
    "liheap_enhancement_charge",
    "ee_and_srp_uncollectible_charge",
    "transition_restructuring",
    "pension_adjustment_factor",
    "performance_incentive_factor",
    "revenue_decoupling_rdm",
)
_renewable_energy_keys = (
    "long_term_contracting_charge",
    "net_metering_charge",
    "renewables_charge",
    "re_growth_charge",
)
_rie_pre_labeled = _built_rr.with_columns(
    pl.when(pl.col("program_key") == "customer_charge")
    .then(pl.lit("Customer Charge"))
    .when(pl.col("program_key") == "core_delivery_rate")
    .then(pl.lit("Distribution Charge"))
    .when(
        pl.col("program_key").is_in(
            ["transmission_charge", "transmission_uncollectible"],
        ),
    )
    .then(pl.lit("Transmission Charge"))
    .when(pl.col("program_key").is_in(_other_delivery_keys))
    .then(pl.lit("Other Delivery Charge"))
    .when(pl.col("program_key").is_in(_renewable_energy_keys))
    .then(pl.lit("Renewable Energy"))
    .when(pl.col("program_key") == "energy_efficiency_programs_charge")
    .then(pl.lit("Energy Efficiency"))
    .otherwise(
        pl.col("program_key").str.replace("_", " ").str.to_titlecase(),
    )
    .alias("program_label"),
)
_rie_pre = _rie_pre_labeled.filter(
    (pl.col("program_key") != "performance_incentive_factor")
    & (pl.col("total_budget") > 0),
)
rie_budget_df = (
    _rie_pre.group_by("program_label")
    .agg(
        pl.col("total_budget").sum(),
        pl.col("charge_unit").n_unique().alias("_nu_cu"),
        pl.col("charge_unit").first().alias("_v_cu"),
        pl.col("rate_comment").n_unique().alias("_nu_rc"),
        pl.col("rate_comment").first().alias("_v_rc"),
        pl.col("bucket_label").n_unique().alias("_nu_bl"),
        pl.col("bucket_label").first().alias("_v_bl"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("_nu_cu") == 1)
        .then(pl.col("_v_cu"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("—"))
        .alias("charge_unit"),
        pl.when(pl.col("_nu_rc") == 1)
        .then(pl.col("_v_rc"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("—"))
        .alias("rate_comment"),
        pl.when(pl.col("_nu_bl") == 1)
        .then(pl.col("_v_bl"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("—"))
        .alias("bucket_label"),
    )
    .drop("_nu_cu", "_v_cu", "_nu_rc", "_v_rc", "_nu_bl", "_v_bl")
)
_RIE_RR_CHART_LABEL_ORDER = (
    "Customer Charge",
    "Distribution Charge",
    "Transmission Charge",
    "Other Delivery Charge",
    "Renewable Energy",
    "Energy Efficiency",
)
# Stacked bar segments and first-row Area cells in the program table (same fills).
# Bar order L→R: sky, midnight, pistachio, gray, saffron; EE uses carrot (6th bucket).
_RIE_RR_FILL: dict[str, str] = {
    "Customer Charge": SB_COLORS["sky"],
    "Distribution Charge": SB_COLORS["midnight"],
    "Transmission Charge": SB_COLORS["pistachio"],
    "Other Delivery Charge": SB_COLORS["gray"],
    "Renewable Energy": SB_COLORS["saffron"],
    "Energy Efficiency": SB_COLORS["carrot"],
}
# Contrasting label text on those fills (matches in-bar label colors).
_RIE_RR_BUCKET_TEXT: dict[str, str] = {
    "Customer Charge": SB_COLORS["midnight"],
    "Distribution Charge": SB_COLORS["white"],
    "Transmission Charge": SB_COLORS["white"],
    "Other Delivery Charge": SB_COLORS["white"],
    "Renewable Energy": SB_COLORS["midnight"],
    "Energy Efficiency": SB_COLORS["midnight"],
}
_present_rr = set(rie_budget_df["program_label"].to_list())
rie_rr_chart_label_order = [x for x in _RIE_RR_CHART_LABEL_ORDER if x in _present_rr]
_extras_rr = _present_rr - set(rie_rr_chart_label_order)
if _extras_rr:
    rie_rr_chart_label_order = rie_rr_chart_label_order + sorted(_extras_rr)
rie_budget_df = rie_budget_df.join(
    pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "program_label": rie_rr_chart_label_order,
            "_rie_rr_sort": list(range(len(rie_rr_chart_label_order))),
        },
    ),
    on="program_label",
    how="left",
).sort("_rie_rr_sort")
rie_pos_sum = float(rie_budget_df["total_budget"].sum())
rie_budget_df = rie_budget_df.with_columns(
    (100.0 * pl.col("total_budget") / pl.lit(rie_pos_sum)).alias("pct_of_positive_line_items"),
).drop("_rie_rr_sort")

RIE_TEST_YEAR_KWH = float(_rev_yaml["test_year_residential_kwh"])


def _rie_fmt_money_short(v: float) -> str:
    av = abs(float(v))
    if av >= 1_000_000:
        s = f"{v / 1_000_000:.2f}".rstrip("0").rstrip(".")
        return f"${s}M"
    if av >= 1000:
        s = f"{v / 1000:.2f}".rstrip("0").rstrip(".")
        return f"${s}k"
    return f"${v:,.0f}"


def _rie_fmt_customer_mo(monthly_usd: float) -> str:
    """Per-customer monthly charge for in-bar labels (e.g. $6/mo, $3.22/mo)."""
    v = float(monthly_usd)
    if abs(v - round(v)) < 1e-4 and abs(v) < 100:
        return f"${int(round(v))}/mo"
    return f"${v:.2f}/mo"


def _rie_parse_fixed_mo_comment(comment: str) -> str | None:
    """YAML `charge_unit` trailing comment like `# $6.00/month` → `$6/mo`."""
    if not comment:
        return None
    m = re.search(
        r"\$\s*([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*/\s*month",
        comment,
        flags=re.IGNORECASE,
    )
    if not m:
        return None
    val = float(m.group(1).replace(",", ""))
    return _rie_fmt_customer_mo(val)


def _rie_rate_display_for_table(charge_unit: str, rate_comment: str) -> str:
    """GT Rate column: volumetric → ¢/kWh; monthly → `$`/mo from comment; else raw comment."""
    cu = (charge_unit or "").lower()
    rc = (rate_comment or "").strip()
    if not rc:
        return "—"
    if "month" in cu:
        mo = _rie_parse_fixed_mo_comment(rc)
        return mo if mo else rc
    if "kwh" in cu:
        m = re.search(r"(\(?)\s*\$?\s*([\d.]+)\s*/\s*kwh", rc, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
        if not m:
            return rc
        neg = bool(m.group(1))
        usd = float(m.group(2))
        if neg:
            usd = -abs(usd)
        cents = 100.0 * abs(usd)
        if usd < 0:
            return f"-{cents:.2f}¢/kWh"
        return f"{cents:.2f}¢/kWh"
    return rc


def _rie_bucket_rate_sublabel(g: pl.DataFrame, *, kwh: float, customers: float) -> str:
    """Rate lines: blended ¢/kWh first when both vol and fixed exist; then per-customer $/mo (not utility-wide monthly pool)."""
    fixed = g.filter(pl.col("charge_unit").str.contains("month"))
    vol = g.filter(pl.col("charge_unit").str.contains("kWh"))
    lines: list[str] = []
    if vol.height > 0:
        vs = float(vol["total_budget"].sum())
        if vs > 0 and kwh > 0:
            lines.append(f"{100.0 * vs / kwh:.2f}¢/kWh")
    if fixed.height > 0:
        n_fixed = fixed.height
        for row in fixed.select(["total_budget", "rate_comment", "customer_count"]).iter_rows(named=True):
            fs = float(row["total_budget"])
            if fs <= 0:
                continue
            rc = str(row["rate_comment"] or "")
            mo_lbl = _rie_parse_fixed_mo_comment(rc)
            if mo_lbl is None:
                cc_raw = row["customer_count"]
                if cc_raw is not None and float(cc_raw) > 0:
                    mo_lbl = _rie_fmt_customer_mo(fs / float(cc_raw) / 12.0)
                elif n_fixed == 1 and customers > 0:
                    mo_lbl = _rie_fmt_customer_mo(fs / customers / 12.0)
            if mo_lbl is not None:
                lines.append(mo_lbl)
    return "\n".join(lines)


_rie_rr_sublbl_rows: list[dict] = []
for _b in _RIE_RR_CHART_LABEL_ORDER:
    _g = _rie_pre.filter(pl.col("program_label") == _b)
    if _g.height == 0:
        continue
    _rie_rr_sublbl_rows.append(
        {
            "program_label": _b,
            "budget_rate_sublbl": _rie_bucket_rate_sublabel(
                _g,
                kwh=RIE_TEST_YEAR_KWH,
                customers=TEST_YEAR_CUSTOMER_COUNT,
            ),
        },
    )
rie_budget_df = rie_budget_df.join(
    pl.DataFrame(_rie_rr_sublbl_rows),
    on="program_label",
    how="left",
).with_columns(pl.col("budget_rate_sublbl").fill_null(""))

_RIE_PROGRAM_DISPLAY: dict[str, str] = {
    "customer_charge": "Customer charge",
    "core_delivery_rate": "Core delivery rate",
    "transmission_charge": "Transmission charge",
    "transmission_uncollectible": "Transmission uncollectible",
    "long_term_contracting_charge": "Long-term contracting",
    "net_metering_charge": "Net metering",
    "energy_efficiency_programs_charge": "Energy efficiency programs",
    "system_reliability_plan_charge": "System reliability plan",
    "renewables_charge": "Renewables charge",
    "ee_and_srp_uncollectible_charge": "EE & SRP uncollectible",
    "transition_restructuring": "Transition / restructuring",
    "o_m_charge": "O&M",
    "storm_fund_replenishment_factor": "Storm fund replenishment",
    "arrears_arrearage_recovery": "Arrears recovery",
    "low_income_discounts": "Low-income discounts",
    "re_growth_charge": "Renewable energy growth program",
    "liheap_enhancement_charge": "LIHEAP enhancement",
    "capex_revenue": "Capex revenue",
    "pension_adjustment_factor": "Pension adjustment",
    "performance_incentive_factor": "Performance incentive",
}


def _rie_program_display_name(program_key: str) -> str:
    return _RIE_PROGRAM_DISPLAY.get(
        program_key,
        program_key.replace("_", " ").title(),
    )


_bucket_ord_detail = pl.DataFrame(
    {
        "area": list(_RIE_RR_CHART_LABEL_ORDER),
        "_rie_detail_bo": list(range(len(_RIE_RR_CHART_LABEL_ORDER))),
    },
)
_rie_rr_detail_sorted = (
    _rie_pre_labeled.rename({"program_label": "area"})
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("program_key")
        .map_elements(_rie_program_display_name, return_dtype=pl.Utf8)
        .alias("charge"),
    )
    .join(_bucket_ord_detail, on="area", how="left")
    .with_columns(pl.col("_rie_detail_bo").fill_null(99))
    .sort(["_rie_detail_bo", "total_budget"], descending=[False, True])
)
_rie_rr_rate_display = [
    _rie_rate_display_for_table(str(cu or ""), str(rc or ""))
    for cu, rc in zip(
        _rie_rr_detail_sorted["charge_unit"],
        _rie_rr_detail_sorted["rate_comment"],
        strict=True,
    )
]
_rie_delivery_sources_by_pk = _rie_parse_delivery_program_sources(_RAW_REV_YAML)
_rie_rr_sources_md = [
    _rie_format_sources_md(
        _rie_delivery_sources_by_pk.get(str(pk), {}),
        rate_display=rd,
    )
    for pk, rd in zip(
        _rie_rr_detail_sorted["program_key"],
        _rie_rr_rate_display,
        strict=True,
    )
]
rie_rr_program_detail_df = _rie_rr_detail_sorted.with_columns(
    pl.Series("rate_display", _rie_rr_rate_display),
    pl.Series("sources_md", _rie_rr_sources_md),
    pl.col("total_budget").alias("total_revenue"),
).select("area", "charge", "rate_display", "total_revenue", "sources_md")
# Area column: show label only on the first row of each contiguous block (`_area_key` keeps the bucket).
rie_rr_program_detail_df = (
    rie_rr_program_detail_df.with_columns(pl.col("area").alias("_area_key"))
    .with_columns(
        pl.when(
            pl.col("_area_key").shift(1).is_not_null()
            & (pl.col("_area_key").shift(1) == pl.col("_area_key")),
        )
        .then(pl.lit(""))
        .otherwise(pl.col("_area_key"))
        .alias("area"),
    )
)

report_vars["rie_rev_req_n_program_rows"] = int(rie_rr_program_detail_df.height)
report_vars["rie_rev_req_positive_line_items_sum"] = rie_pos_sum

rie_budget_df.select(
    "bucket_label",
    "program_label",
    "charge_unit",
    "rate_comment",
    "total_budget",
    pl.col("pct_of_positive_line_items").round(2),
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rie-rev-req-budgets
#| fig-cap: "RIE delivery revenue collected during the test year (9/1/2024–8/31/2025), by program area. Bars show total revenue collected by area, as well as total volumetric and/or fixed charges used to collect it."
#| fig-cap-location: margin

from plotnine import (
    aes,
    annotate,
    coord_flip,
    element_blank,
    geom_col,
    geom_text,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    position_stack,
    scale_fill_manual,
    scale_x_discrete,
    scale_y_continuous,
    theme,
)

from lib.plotnine import SB_COLORS, theme_switchbox
from lib.quarto import display_svg

_prog_order = rie_rr_chart_label_order


def _rie_usd_compact(v: float) -> str:
    av = abs(float(v))
    if av >= 1_000_000:
        s = f"{v / 1_000_000:.2f}".rstrip("0").rstrip(".")
        return f"${s}M"
    return f"${v:,.0f}"


def _rie_usd_total_annotation(v: float) -> str:
    if v >= 1_000_000:
        s = f"{v / 1_000_000:.2f}".rstrip("0").rstrip(".")
        return f"${s}M"
    return f"${v:,.0f}"


_RIE_RR_BAR_SHORT = {
    "Customer Charge": "Customer\nCharge",
    "Distribution Charge": "Distribution",
    "Transmission Charge": "Transmission",
    "Other Delivery Charge": "Other delivery",
    "Renewable Energy": "Renewable\nEnergy",
    "Energy Efficiency": "Energy\nEfficiency",
}

_plot_df = (
    rie_budget_df.with_columns(pl.col("program_label").cast(pl.Enum(_prog_order)))
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("total_budget")
        .map_elements(_rie_usd_compact, return_dtype=pl.Utf8)
        .alias("_budget_top"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("budget_rate_sublbl") != "")
        .then(pl.col("_budget_top") + pl.lit("\n\n") + pl.col("budget_rate_sublbl"))
        .otherwise(pl.col("_budget_top"))
        .alias("budget_lbl"),
    )
    .drop("_budget_top")
)
_fill_colors = {p: _RIE_RR_FILL[p] for p in _prog_order}

_in_bar = _plot_df.filter(pl.col("pct_of_positive_line_items") >= 2.0)

cum_pct = 0.0
_rie_bar_annos: list = []
for _lbl in _prog_order:
    _row = _plot_df.filter(pl.col("program_label") == _lbl)
    if _row.height == 0:
        continue
    _p = float(_row["pct_of_positive_line_items"][0])
    _mid = cum_pct + _p / 2.0
    cum_pct += _p
    _rie_bar_annos.append(
        annotate(
            "text",
            x=1.42,
            y=_mid,
            label=_RIE_RR_BAR_SHORT.get(_lbl, _lbl),
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            size=8,
            color=_RIE_RR_FILL[_lbl],
            fontweight="normal",
        ),
    )

_total_lbl = _rie_usd_total_annotation(rie_rr_total)
_rie_rr_plot_title = (
    f"Rhode Island Energy collected {_total_lbl} in revenue between 9/1/2024 and 8/31/2025"
)

p_rie_rr = (
    ggplot(_plot_df, aes(x="'Delivery revenue'", y="pct_of_positive_line_items", fill="program_label"))
    + geom_col(
        position=position_stack(reverse=True),
        width=0.8,
        colour=None,
        size=0,
    )
    + geom_text(
        _in_bar,
        aes(label="budget_lbl"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        size=5,
        fontweight="bold",
        color="white",
        ha="center",
        lineheight=1.08,
    )
    + coord_flip()
    + scale_fill_manual(values=_fill_colors, breaks=_prog_order, guide=None)
    + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0, 0.35, 0, 1.15))
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.08, 0))
    + labs(title=_rie_rr_plot_title, x="", y="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(10.5, 3.65),
        axis_text=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_x=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_y=element_blank(),
        axis_line=element_blank(),
        axis_line_x=element_blank(),
        panel_grid=element_blank(),
        legend_position="none",
    )
)
for _a in _rie_bar_annos:
    p_rie_rr = p_rie_rr + _a


def _rie_style_inbar_budget_labels(fig, *, top_pt: float = 6, sub_pt: float = 5, gap_pt: float = 9) -> None:
    """Larger font for bucket $ total; rate lines stay smaller (plotnine has one size per geom_text)."""
    import matplotlib.colors as mcolors

    ax = fig.axes[0]

    def _is_near_white(c) -> bool:
        try:
            r, g, b, *_ = mcolors.to_rgba(c)
        except ValueError:
            return False
        return r > 0.9 and g > 0.9 and b > 0.9

    for t in list(ax.texts):
        s = t.get_text()
        if not s:
            continue
        if not _is_near_white(t.get_color()):
            continue
        w = t.get_fontweight()
        if w not in ("bold", "heavy") and not (isinstance(w, (int, float)) and w >= 600):
            continue
        if "\n\n" in s and ("/mo" in s or "/kWh" in s):
            top, rest = s.split("\n\n", 1)
            x, y = t.get_position()
            col = t.get_color()
            t.remove()
            ax.annotate(
                top,
                xy=(x, y),
                xytext=(0, gap_pt),
                textcoords="offset points",
                ha="center",
                va="bottom",
                fontsize=top_pt,
                fontweight="bold",
                color=col,
            )
            ax.annotate(
                rest,
                xy=(x, y),
                xytext=(0, -gap_pt),
                textcoords="offset points",
                ha="center",
                va="top",
                fontsize=sub_pt,
                fontweight="bold",
                color=col,
                linespacing=1.08,
            )
        elif "\n" not in s and s.startswith("$"):
            t.set_fontsize(top_pt)


fig = p_rie_rr.draw()
_rie_style_inbar_budget_labels(fig)
display_svg(fig)
```

### Program line items by area

Each delivery line from the YAML appears below with the same six **areas** as the stacked bar. The **Area** label appears **once per section** (first program row in that bucket); additional rows leave Area blank. The labeled cell uses the **same fill and label colors** as the stacked-bar segment. **Rate** is **¢/kWh** or per-customer **`$/mo`** from filing comments. **Sources** lists **Rate** and **Revenue** links into RIE Book 21 on DocumentCloud (same exhibit when both apply), plus **YAML `#` notes** only when they add something beyond the Rate and Revenue columns (e.g. a non-trivial revenue derivation).

```{python}
#| label: tbl-rie-rev-req-programs

from lib.great_tables import get_switchbox_gt_tab_options
from lib.plotnine import SB_COLORS

_rie_rr_program_gt_df = rie_rr_program_detail_df.drop("_area_key")

_gt_rr_prog = (
    GT(_rie_rr_program_gt_df)
    .tab_header(
        title="Delivery charges and revenue, by area",
        subtitle=(
            "Rates and revenue collected during the rate-case test year (9/1/24–8/31/25); "
        ),
    )
    .cols_label(
        area="Area",
        charge="Charge",
        rate_display="Rate",
        total_revenue="Revenue",
        sources_md="Sources",
    )
    .fmt_currency(columns="total_revenue", currency="USD", decimals=0)
    .fmt_markdown(columns="sources_md")
    .cols_align(align="left", columns=["area", "charge", "rate_display", "sources_md"])
    .cols_align(align="right", columns="total_revenue")
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
for _i, _key in enumerate(rie_rr_program_detail_df["_area_key"]):
    _key = str(_key)
    _area_shown = str(rie_rr_program_detail_df["area"][_i])
    if not _area_shown:
        _gt_rr_prog = _gt_rr_prog.tab_style(
            style=[
                style.fill(color=SB_COLORS["white"]),
                style.text(color="#4D4D4D", weight="normal"),
            ],
            locations=loc.body(columns="area", rows=[_i]),
        )
        continue
    _bg = _RIE_RR_FILL.get(_key, SB_COLORS["gray_light"])
    _fg = _RIE_RR_BUCKET_TEXT.get(_key, SB_COLORS["midnight"])
    _gt_rr_prog = _gt_rr_prog.tab_style(
        style=[
            style.fill(color=_bg),
            style.text(color=_fg, weight="bold"),
        ],
        locations=loc.body(columns="area", rows=[_i]),
    )

Path("../cache").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
Path("../cache/rie_rev_req_programs_by_bucket.html").write_text(_gt_rr_prog.as_raw_html(), encoding="utf-8")
_gt_rr_prog
```

### HP rate comparison tables (by area and by line item)

The HP flat rate applies a **uniform volumetric discount** to all delivery components. We compute the discount ratio from the calibrated tariffs and apply it to every volumetric line item. Per-customer charges are unchanged.

```{python}
#| label: hp-rate-comparison-tables

_hp_discount_ratio = _hp_flat_vol_usd_kwh / _default_vol_usd_kwh
_hp_pct_reduction = 1.0 - _hp_discount_ratio

_nonhp_default_vol_usd_kwh = _rie_calibrated_volumetric_usd_per_kwh(
    rel_filename="rie_nonhp_default_calibrated.json",
)
_adj_default_ratio = _nonhp_default_vol_usd_kwh / _default_vol_usd_kwh
_adj_default_pct_increase = _adj_default_ratio - 1.0

# --- Shared: area-level volumetric budgets ---

_vol_areas = (
    _rie_rr_detail_sorted
    .filter(pl.col("charge_unit").str.to_lowercase().str.contains("kwh"))
    .group_by("area")
    .agg(pl.col("total_budget").sum())
)
_area_order_names = [a for a in _RIE_RR_CHART_LABEL_ORDER if a != "Customer Charge"]
_area_order_df = pl.DataFrame(
    {"area": _area_order_names, "_ao": list(range(len(_area_order_names)))},
)
_vol_areas = (
    _vol_areas.join(_area_order_df, on="area", how="left")
    .with_columns(pl.col("_ao").fill_null(99))
    .sort("_ao")
    .drop("_ao")
)


def _build_area_gt(
    ratio: float,
    pct_change: float,
    new_total_c: float,
    *,
    col_label: str,
    change_label: str,
    change_fmt: str,
    cache_filename: str,
) -> GT:
    """Build an area-level rate comparison GT (HP or adjusted default)."""
    _area_display = {"Transmission Charge": "Transmission", "Other Delivery Charge": "Other Delivery"}
    rows: list[dict] = []
    for row in _vol_areas.iter_rows(named=True):
        default_c = 100.0 * row["total_budget"] / _ty_kwh
        new_c = default_c * ratio
        rows.append({
            "area": _area_display.get(row["area"], row["area"]),
            "default_rate": default_c,
            "new_rate": new_c,
            "pct_change": pct_change * 100.0,
        })
    rows.append({
        "area": "Total",
        "default_rate": _default_vol_usd_kwh * 100.0,
        "new_rate": new_total_c * 100.0,
        "pct_change": pct_change * 100.0,
    })
    df = pl.DataFrame(rows)

    gt = (
        GT(df)
        .cols_label(
            area="Area",
            default_rate="Default rate",
            new_rate=col_label,
            pct_change=change_label,
        )
        .fmt_number(columns=["default_rate", "new_rate"], decimals=2, pattern="{x}¢/kWh")
        .fmt_number(columns="pct_change", decimals=1, pattern=change_fmt)
        .cols_align(align="left", columns="area")
        .cols_align(align="right", columns=["default_rate", "new_rate", "pct_change"])
        .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options(
            table_layout="auto",
            extra_table_additional_css=[
                ".table-striped > tbody > tr:nth-of-type(odd) > * { --bs-table-striped-bg: transparent !important; }",
            ],
        ))
    )
    _area_display_rev = {v: k for k, v in _area_display.items()}
    for i, a in enumerate(df["area"].to_list()):
        a = str(a)
        if a == "Total":
            gt = gt.tab_style(
                style=[style.text(weight="bold")],
                locations=loc.body(rows=[i]),
            )
            continue
        _orig = _area_display_rev.get(a, a)
        bg = _RIE_RR_FILL.get(_orig, SB_COLORS["gray_light"])
        fg = _RIE_RR_BUCKET_TEXT.get(_orig, SB_COLORS["midnight"])
        gt = gt.tab_style(
            style=[style.fill(color=bg), style.text(color=fg, weight="bold")],
            locations=loc.body(columns="area", rows=[i]),
        )

    Path(f"../cache/{cache_filename}").write_text(gt.as_raw_html(), encoding="utf-8")
    return gt


# --- Table 1a: HP rate by area ---
_gt_area_hp = _build_area_gt(
    _hp_discount_ratio, _hp_pct_reduction, _hp_flat_vol_usd_kwh,
    col_label="HP rate",
    change_label="Change",
    change_fmt="-{x}%",
    cache_filename="rate_comparison_by_area.html",
)
_gt_area_hp
```

```{python}
#| label: adj-default-rate-comparison-by-area

# --- Table 1b: adjusted default rate by area ---
_gt_area_adj = _build_area_gt(
    _adj_default_ratio, _adj_default_pct_increase, _nonhp_default_vol_usd_kwh,
    col_label="Adj. default rate",
    change_label="Change",
    change_fmt="+{x}%",
    cache_filename="rate_comparison_adj_default_by_area.html",
)
_gt_area_adj
```

```{python}
#| label: hp-rate-comparison-line-items

# --- Table 2: full line-item detail (for exhibit) ---

_detail_with_rates = _rie_rr_detail_sorted.with_columns(
    pl.Series("rate_display", _rie_rr_rate_display),
)
_is_vol = _detail_with_rates["charge_unit"].str.to_lowercase().str.contains("kwh")

_hp_rate_display: list[str] = []
_reduction_display: list[str] = []
_adj_default_display: list[str] = []
_adj_change_display: list[str] = []
for _idx in range(_detail_with_rates.height):
    _rd = str(_detail_with_rates["rate_display"][_idx])
    if _is_vol[_idx]:
        _budget = float(_detail_with_rates["total_budget"][_idx])
        _default_c = 100.0 * _budget / _ty_kwh
        _hp_c = _default_c * _hp_discount_ratio
        _adj_c = _default_c * _adj_default_ratio
        if _default_c < 0:
            _hp_rate_display.append(f"-{abs(_hp_c):.2f}¢/kWh")
            _adj_default_display.append(f"-{abs(_adj_c):.2f}¢/kWh")
        else:
            _hp_rate_display.append(f"{_hp_c:.2f}¢/kWh")
            _adj_default_display.append(f"{_adj_c:.2f}¢/kWh")
        _reduction_display.append(f"-{_hp_pct_reduction * 100.0:.1f}%")
        _adj_change_display.append(f"+{_adj_default_pct_increase * 100.0:.1f}%")
    else:
        _hp_rate_display.append(_rd)
        _reduction_display.append("—")
        _adj_default_display.append(_rd)
        _adj_change_display.append("—")

_detail_for_gt = (
    _detail_with_rates.select("area", "charge", "rate_display")
    .with_columns(
        pl.Series("hp_rate_display", _hp_rate_display),
        pl.Series("reduction_display", _reduction_display),
        pl.Series("adj_default_display", _adj_default_display),
        pl.Series("adj_change_display", _adj_change_display),
    )
)

# Area column: show label only on first row of each contiguous block.
_detail_for_gt = _detail_for_gt.with_columns(
    pl.col("area").alias("_area_key"),
).with_columns(
    pl.when(
        pl.col("_area_key").shift(1).is_not_null()
        & (pl.col("_area_key").shift(1) == pl.col("_area_key")),
    )
    .then(pl.lit(""))
    .otherwise(pl.col("area"))
    .alias("area"),
)

# Add a Total row.
_total_row = pl.DataFrame([{
    "area": "Total",
    "charge": "",
    "rate_display": f"{_default_vol_usd_kwh * 100.0:.2f}¢/kWh",
    "hp_rate_display": f"{_hp_flat_vol_usd_kwh * 100.0:.2f}¢/kWh",
    "reduction_display": f"-{_hp_pct_reduction * 100.0:.1f}%",
    "adj_default_display": f"{_nonhp_default_vol_usd_kwh * 100.0:.2f}¢/kWh",
    "adj_change_display": f"+{_adj_default_pct_increase * 100.0:.1f}%",
    "_area_key": "Total",
}])
_detail_for_gt = pl.concat([_detail_for_gt, _total_row], how="diagonal")

_gt_detail_df = _detail_for_gt.drop("_area_key")
_gt_detail_comp = (
    GT(_gt_detail_df)
    .tab_header(
        title="Delivery rates: default, proposed HP, and adjusted default schedules",
        subtitle="Per-customer charges are unchanged. HP volumetric charges are reduced; non-HP volumetric charges are increased to maintain revenue neutrality.",
    )
    .cols_label(
        area="Area",
        charge="Charge",
        rate_display="Default rate",
        hp_rate_display="HP rate",
        reduction_display="HP change",
        adj_default_display="Adj. default rate",
        adj_change_display="Adj. change",
    )
    .cols_align(align="left", columns=["area", "charge"])
    .cols_align(align="right", columns=["rate_display", "hp_rate_display", "reduction_display", "adj_default_display", "adj_change_display"])
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options(
        extra_table_additional_css=[
            ".table-striped > tbody > tr:nth-of-type(odd) > * { --bs-table-striped-bg: transparent !important; }",
        ],
    ))
)

# Color area cells (same logic as the programs-by-bucket table).
for _i in range(_detail_for_gt.height):
    _key = str(_detail_for_gt["_area_key"][_i])
    _area_shown = str(_detail_for_gt["area"][_i])
    if _key == "Total":
        _gt_detail_comp = _gt_detail_comp.tab_style(
            style=[style.text(weight="bold")],
            locations=loc.body(rows=[_i]),
        )
        continue
    if not _area_shown:
        _gt_detail_comp = _gt_detail_comp.tab_style(
            style=[
                style.fill(color=SB_COLORS["white"]),
                style.text(color="#4D4D4D", weight="normal"),
            ],
            locations=loc.body(columns="area", rows=[_i]),
        )
        continue
    _bg = _RIE_RR_FILL.get(_key, SB_COLORS["gray_light"])
    _fg = _RIE_RR_BUCKET_TEXT.get(_key, SB_COLORS["midnight"])
    _gt_detail_comp = _gt_detail_comp.tab_style(
        style=[style.fill(color=_bg), style.text(color=_fg, weight="bold")],
        locations=loc.body(columns="area", rows=[_i]),
    )

Path("../cache/rate_comparison_line_items.html").write_text(
    _gt_detail_comp.as_raw_html(), encoding="utf-8",
)

report_vars["hp_discount_ratio"] = _hp_discount_ratio
report_vars["hp_pct_reduction"] = _hp_pct_reduction
report_vars["default_vol_usd_kwh"] = _default_vol_usd_kwh
report_vars["nonhp_adj_default_vol_usd_kwh"] = _nonhp_default_vol_usd_kwh
report_vars["adj_default_pct_increase"] = _adj_default_pct_increase

_fixed_charges = (
    _detail_with_rates
    .filter(~_is_vol)
    .select("charge", "rate_display")
)
_fc_parts = [
    f"{row['rate_display']} for the {row['charge'].lower()}"
    for row in _fixed_charges.iter_rows(named=True)
]
report_vars["fixed_charges_description"] = ", ".join(_fc_parts)

_n_vol_line_items = int(_is_vol.sum())
_n_dist_vol = int(
    _detail_with_rates.filter(_is_vol)
    .filter(pl.col("area") == "Distribution Charge")
    .height
)
report_vars["n_other_vol_line_items"] = _n_vol_line_items - _n_dist_vol

_gt_detail_comp
```

## Load, validate, and aggregate (both scenarios)

We `scan_parquet` each master BAT root with `hive_partitioning=True` (same as `analysis.qmd`), filter to RIE, derive **cost of service (delivery)**, then **validate** weighted totals against the YAML and **aggregate** by `heating_type_v2` with the spine + integer display counts. The cell below defines small helpers and runs the pipeline twice: **`bat` / `cos_tbl`** for **run 1+2**, **`bat_hp_flat` / `cos_tbl_hp_flat`** for **run 17+18**.

```{python}
#| label: bat-pipeline

import numpy as np

from lib.great_tables import get_switchbox_gt_tab_options

# Switchbox fonts / sizes for Great Tables (GT Planar headers, IBM Plex data); see lib/great_tables/.
_COS_GT_TAB_OPTS = get_switchbox_gt_tab_options()


def _cos_gt_center_cells(gt: GT, *, data_columns: list[str]) -> GT:
    return (
        gt.cols_align(align="center", columns=data_columns)
        .tab_style(
            style=style.text(align="center"),
            locations=loc.stub(),
        )
        .tab_style(
            style=style.text(align="center"),
            locations=loc.column_labels(),
        )
    )


def load_master_bat(path_master_bat: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return (
        pl.scan_parquet(path_master_bat, hive_partitioning=True)
        .filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY)
        .select(
            "bldg_id",
            "weight",
            "postprocess_group.heating_type_v2",
            "annual_bill_delivery",
            "economic_burden_delivery",
            "residual_share_epmc_delivery",
            "BAT_epmc_delivery",
        )
        .with_columns(
            (
                pl.col("economic_burden_delivery")
                + pl.col("residual_share_epmc_delivery")
            ).alias("cost_of_service_delivery"),
        )
        .collect()
    )


def assert_bill_identity(bat: pl.DataFrame, scenario: str) -> None:
    _check = bat.with_columns(
        (
            pl.col("economic_burden_delivery")
            + pl.col("residual_share_epmc_delivery")
            + pl.col("BAT_epmc_delivery")
            - pl.col("annual_bill_delivery")
        )
        .abs()
        .alias("bill_identity_abs_err"),
    )
    _mx = float(_check["bill_identity_abs_err"].max())
    assert _mx < 1e-2, (
        f"{scenario}: annual_bill_delivery should equal EB + EPMC residual + BAT_epmc "
        f"(max err {_mx})"
    )


def validate_totals(bat: pl.DataFrame, scenario: str, *, hp_flat: bool) -> None:
    total_w = float(bat["weight"].sum())
    total_rev = float((bat["weight"] * bat["annual_bill_delivery"]).sum())
    total_cos = float((bat["weight"] * bat["cost_of_service_delivery"]).sum())
    total_xs = float((bat["weight"] * bat["BAT_epmc_delivery"]).sum())

    assert abs(total_w - TEST_YEAR_CUSTOMER_COUNT) < 0.05, (
        f"{scenario}: sum(weight)={total_w} vs test_year_customer_count="
        f"{TEST_YEAR_CUSTOMER_COUNT}"
    )
    assert abs(total_rev - TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT) < 2000.0, (
        f"{scenario}: sum(w×delivery)={total_rev} vs RR={TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT}"
    )
    assert abs(total_xs) < 5000.0, (
        f"{scenario}: cross-subsidy total should net ~0, got {total_xs}"
    )
    assert abs(total_cos - TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT) < 5000.0, (
        f"{scenario}: sum(w×COS)={total_cos} vs delivery RR="
        f"{TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT}"
    )

    if hp_flat:
        report_vars["cos_subclass_hp_flat_total_customers"] = total_w
        report_vars["cos_subclass_hp_flat_total_delivery_rev"] = total_rev
        report_vars["cos_subclass_hp_flat_total_cos"] = total_cos
    else:
        report_vars["cos_subclass_total_customers"] = total_w
        report_vars["cos_subclass_total_delivery_rev"] = total_rev
        report_vars["cos_subclass_total_cos"] = total_cos


def build_cos_table(bat: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    total_w = float(bat["weight"].sum())
    total_rev = float((bat["weight"] * bat["annual_bill_delivery"]).sum())

    _by_ht = bat.group_by("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2").agg(
        pl.col("weight").sum().alias("n_customers"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("annual_bill_delivery"))
        .sum()
        .alias("revenue_delivery"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("cost_of_service_delivery"))
        .sum()
        .alias("cost_of_service"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("BAT_epmc_delivery"))
        .sum()
        .alias("cross_subsidy"),
    )

    _spine = pl.DataFrame({"postprocess_group.heating_type_v2": list(HT_V2_ORDER)})
    _agg_cols = [
        "subclass",
        "n_customers",
        "revenue_delivery",
        "cost_of_service",
        "cross_subsidy",
    ]
    by_ht = (
        _spine.join(_by_ht, on="postprocess_group.heating_type_v2", how="left")
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("n_customers").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("revenue_delivery").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("cost_of_service").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("cross_subsidy").fill_null(0.0),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2")
            .replace(HT_V2_LABELS)
            .alias("subclass"),
        )
        .drop("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2")
        .select(_agg_cols)
    )

    _total = pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "subclass": ["All customers"],
            "n_customers": [float(by_ht["n_customers"].sum())],
            "revenue_delivery": [float(by_ht["revenue_delivery"].sum())],
            "cost_of_service": [float(by_ht["cost_of_service"].sum())],
            "cross_subsidy": [float(by_ht["cross_subsidy"].sum())],
        }
    ).select(_agg_cols)

    cos_tbl = (
        pl.concat([by_ht, _total], how="vertical")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("n_customers") / total_w).alias("pct_all_customers"),
            (pl.col("revenue_delivery") / total_rev).alias("pct_all_revenue"),
            (pl.col("cost_of_service") / TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT).alias(
                "pct_revenue_requirement",
            ),
            pl.when(pl.col("cost_of_service") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("cross_subsidy") / pl.col("cost_of_service"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("pct_overpayment_vs_cos"),
        )
    )

    _sub_rows = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") != "All customers")
    _w_sub = _sub_rows["n_customers"].to_numpy()
    _raw = _w_sub * DISPLAY_CUSTOMER_TOTAL / total_w
    _floors = np.floor(_raw).astype(np.int64)
    _rem = DISPLAY_CUSTOMER_TOTAL - int(_floors.sum())
    _order = np.argsort(-(_raw - _floors))
    for _k in range(_rem):
        _floors[_order[_k]] += 1
    _lut_sub = pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "subclass": _sub_rows["subclass"],
            "n_customers_display": _floors,
        }
    )
    _lut_total = pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "subclass": ["All customers"],
            "n_customers_display": np.array([DISPLAY_CUSTOMER_TOTAL], dtype=np.int64),
        }
    )
    return (
        cos_tbl.join(pl.concat([_lut_sub, _lut_total]), on="subclass", how="left")
        .with_columns(
            (
                pl.col("n_customers_display").cast(pl.Float64)
                / float(DISPLAY_CUSTOMER_TOTAL)
            ).alias("pct_customers_display"),
        )
    )


def build_gt_cos_totals(
    cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    title: str = "Delivery revenue and cost of service by residential customer subclass",
    subtitle: str | None = None,
) -> GT:
    _has_kwh = "consumption_gwh" in cos_tbl.columns
    _select_cols = [
        "subclass",
        "n_customers_display",
        "pct_customers_display",
    ]
    if _has_kwh:
        _select_cols.extend(["consumption_gwh", "pct_all_consumption"])
    _select_cols.extend([
        "revenue_delivery",
        "pct_all_revenue",
        "cost_of_service",
        "pct_revenue_requirement",
        "cross_subsidy",
        "pct_overpayment_vs_cos",
    ])
    _gt_df = cos_tbl.select(_select_cols)
    if _has_kwh:
        _gt_df = _gt_df.with_columns(
            pl.col("consumption_gwh")
            .map_elements(lambda v: f"{v:,.1f} GWh", return_dtype=pl.String)
            .alias("consumption_gwh"),
        )
    _labels: dict[str, str] = {
        "n_customers_display": "Customers",
        "pct_customers_display": "% of customers",
        "revenue_delivery": "Delivery revenue",
        "pct_all_revenue": "% of revenue",
        "cost_of_service": "Cost of service",
        "pct_revenue_requirement": "% of cost",
        "cross_subsidy": "Cross-subsidy",
        "pct_overpayment_vs_cos": "Cross-subsidy ÷ COS",
    }
    if _has_kwh:
        _labels["consumption_gwh"] = "Consumption"
        _labels["pct_all_consumption"] = "% of consumption"
    _gt = (
        GT(_gt_df, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(
            title=title,
            subtitle=subtitle,
        )
        .tab_options(**_COS_GT_TAB_OPTS)
        .cols_label(**_labels)
        .fmt_number(columns=["n_customers_display"], decimals=0, use_seps=True)
        .fmt_percent(columns=["pct_customers_display", "pct_all_revenue"], decimals=1)
        .fmt_percent(
            columns=["pct_revenue_requirement", "pct_overpayment_vs_cos"],
            decimals=1,
        )
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=["revenue_delivery", "cost_of_service", "cross_subsidy"],
            decimals=0,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    if _has_kwh:
        _gt = _gt.fmt_percent(columns=["pct_all_consumption"], decimals=1)
    _data_cols = [
        "n_customers_display",
        "pct_customers_display",
    ]
    if _has_kwh:
        _data_cols.extend(["consumption_gwh", "pct_all_consumption"])
    _data_cols.extend([
        "revenue_delivery",
        "pct_all_revenue",
        "cost_of_service",
        "pct_revenue_requirement",
        "cross_subsidy",
        "pct_overpayment_vs_cos",
    ])
    return _cos_gt_center_cells(_gt, data_columns=_data_cols)


def build_gt_cos_totals_hp_flat_vs_current(
    cos_tbl_current: pl.DataFrame,
    cos_tbl_hp_flat: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    subtitle: str,
    label_spanner_today: str,
    label_spanner_proposed_delivery: str,
    label_spanner_cos: str,
    title: str = "Delivery revenue and cost of service by residential customer subclass",
) -> GT:
    """Totals: today's uniform delivery revenue vs proposed split rates; COS/BAT under proposal."""
    _cur = cos_tbl_current.select(
        "subclass",
        pl.col("revenue_delivery").alias("rev_delivery_current"),
        pl.col("pct_all_revenue").alias("pct_rev_delivery_current"),
    )
    _hp = cos_tbl_hp_flat.select(
        "subclass",
        pl.col("revenue_delivery").alias("rev_delivery_hp_flat"),
        pl.col("pct_all_revenue").alias("pct_rev_delivery_hp_flat"),
        "cost_of_service",
        "pct_revenue_requirement",
        "cross_subsidy",
        "pct_overpayment_vs_cos",
    )
    _gt_df = (
        _cur.join(_hp, on="subclass", how="inner")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("rev_delivery_hp_flat") - pl.col("rev_delivery_current")).alias(
                "delta_rev_delivery",
            ),
        )
        .select(
            "subclass",
            "rev_delivery_current",
            "pct_rev_delivery_current",
            "delta_rev_delivery",
            "rev_delivery_hp_flat",
            "pct_rev_delivery_hp_flat",
            "cost_of_service",
            "pct_revenue_requirement",
            "cross_subsidy",
            "pct_overpayment_vs_cos",
        )
    )
    _gt_hp = (
        GT(_gt_df, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(
            title=title,
            subtitle=subtitle,
        )
        .tab_spanner(
            label=label_spanner_today,
            columns=[
                "rev_delivery_current",
                "pct_rev_delivery_current",
            ],
        )
        .tab_spanner(
            label=label_spanner_proposed_delivery,
            columns=[
              "delta_rev_delivery",
              "rev_delivery_hp_flat",
              "pct_rev_delivery_hp_flat",
              "cost_of_service",
              "pct_revenue_requirement",
              "cross_subsidy",
              "pct_overpayment_vs_cos",
              ],
        )
        .cols_label(
            rev_delivery_current="Delivery revenue",
            pct_rev_delivery_current="% of total",
            delta_rev_delivery="Change ($)",
            rev_delivery_hp_flat="Delivery revenue",
            pct_rev_delivery_hp_flat="% of total",
            cost_of_service="Cost of service",
            pct_revenue_requirement="% of rev. requirement",
            cross_subsidy="Cross-subsidy",
            pct_overpayment_vs_cos="Cross-subsidy ÷ COS",
        )
        .tab_options(
            **get_switchbox_gt_tab_options(
                table_layout="auto",
                table_width="1360px",
            ),
        )
        .cols_width(
            subclass="132px",
            rev_delivery_current="142px",
            pct_rev_delivery_current="90px",
            delta_rev_delivery="108px",
            rev_delivery_hp_flat="142px",
            pct_rev_delivery_hp_flat="90px",
            cost_of_service="124px",
            pct_revenue_requirement="102px",
            cross_subsidy="118px",
            pct_overpayment_vs_cos="104px",
        )
        .fmt_percent(
            columns=[
                "pct_rev_delivery_current",
                "pct_rev_delivery_hp_flat",
                "pct_revenue_requirement",
                "pct_overpayment_vs_cos",
            ],
            decimals=1,
        )
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=[
                "rev_delivery_current",
                "delta_rev_delivery",
                "rev_delivery_hp_flat",
                "cost_of_service",
                "cross_subsidy",
            ],
            decimals=0,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    return (
        _cos_gt_center_cells(
            _gt_hp,
            data_columns=[
                "rev_delivery_current",
                "pct_rev_delivery_current",
                "delta_rev_delivery",
                "rev_delivery_hp_flat",
                "pct_rev_delivery_hp_flat",
                "cost_of_service",
                "pct_revenue_requirement",
                "cross_subsidy",
                "pct_overpayment_vs_cos",
            ],
        )
    )


def build_gt_cos_avg(
    cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    subtitle: str,
    title: str = "Average delivery bill, COS, and BAT_epmc by subclass (per customer-year)",
) -> GT:
    _avg_tbl = (
        cos_tbl.select(
            "subclass",
            "n_customers",
            "n_customers_display",
            "pct_customers_display",
            "revenue_delivery",
            "cost_of_service",
            "cross_subsidy",
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("revenue_delivery") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_delivery_bill"),
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("cost_of_service") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_cost_of_service"),
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("cross_subsidy") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_BAT_epmc_delivery"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(
                (pl.col("avg_cost_of_service").is_not_null())
                & (pl.col("avg_cost_of_service") > 0)
            )
            .then(pl.col("avg_BAT_epmc_delivery") / pl.col("avg_cost_of_service"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_bat_div_avg_cos"),
        )
        .select(
            "subclass",
            "n_customers_display",
            "pct_customers_display",
            "avg_delivery_bill",
            "avg_cost_of_service",
            "avg_BAT_epmc_delivery",
            "avg_bat_div_avg_cos",
        )
    )
    _gt = (
        GT(_avg_tbl, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(
            title=title,
            subtitle=subtitle,
        )
        .tab_options(**_COS_GT_TAB_OPTS)
        .cols_label(
            n_customers_display="Customers",
            pct_customers_display="% of customers",
            avg_delivery_bill="Avg. delivery bill",
            avg_cost_of_service="Avg. cost of service",
            avg_BAT_epmc_delivery="Avg. cross-subsidy",
            avg_bat_div_avg_cos="Avg. cross-subsidy ÷ Avg. COS",
        )
        .fmt_number(columns=["n_customers_display"], decimals=0, use_seps=True)
        .fmt_percent(
            columns=["pct_customers_display", "avg_bat_div_avg_cos"],
            decimals=1,
        )
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=["avg_delivery_bill", "avg_cost_of_service", "avg_BAT_epmc_delivery"],
            decimals=0,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    return (
        _cos_gt_center_cells(
            _gt,
            data_columns=[
                "n_customers_display",
                "pct_customers_display",
                "avg_delivery_bill",
                "avg_cost_of_service",
                "avg_BAT_epmc_delivery",
                "avg_bat_div_avg_cos",
            ],
        )
    )


def build_gt_cos_avg_hp_flat_vs_current(
    cos_tbl_current: pl.DataFrame,
    cos_tbl_hp_flat: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    subtitle: str,
    label_spanner_default: str,
    label_spanner_proposed: str,
    title: str = "Average delivery bill, COS, and BAT_epmc by subclass (per customer-year)",
) -> GT:
    """Per-customer averages: default delivery bill + cross-subsidy; then Δ bill and proposed averages."""

    def _avg_side(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
        return (
            df.select(
                "subclass",
                "n_customers",
                "revenue_delivery",
                "cost_of_service",
                "cross_subsidy",
            )
            .with_columns(
                pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
                .then(pl.col("revenue_delivery") / pl.col("n_customers"))
                .otherwise(None)
                .alias("avg_delivery_bill"),
                pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
                .then(pl.col("cost_of_service") / pl.col("n_customers"))
                .otherwise(None)
                .alias("avg_cost_of_service"),
                pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
                .then(pl.col("cross_subsidy") / pl.col("n_customers"))
                .otherwise(None)
                .alias("avg_cross_subsidy"),
            )
            .with_columns(
                pl.when(
                    (pl.col("avg_cost_of_service").is_not_null())
                    & (pl.col("avg_cost_of_service") > 0)
                )
                .then(pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy") / pl.col("avg_cost_of_service"))
                .otherwise(None)
                .alias("avg_bat_div_avg_cos"),
            )
        )

    _c = _avg_side(cos_tbl_current).select(
        "subclass",
        pl.col("avg_delivery_bill").alias("avg_del_default"),
        pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy").alias("avg_xsub_default"),
    )
    _h = _avg_side(cos_tbl_hp_flat).select(
        "subclass",
        pl.col("avg_delivery_bill").alias("avg_del_proposed"),
        pl.col("avg_cost_of_service").alias("avg_cos_proposed"),
        pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy").alias("avg_xsub_proposed"),
        pl.col("avg_bat_div_avg_cos").alias("avg_ratio_proposed"),
    )
    _gt_df = (
        _c.join(_h, on="subclass", how="inner")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("avg_del_proposed") - pl.col("avg_del_default")).alias(
                "delta_avg_delivery",
            ),
        )
        .select(
            "subclass",
            "avg_del_default",
            "avg_xsub_default",
            "delta_avg_delivery",
            "avg_del_proposed",
            "avg_cos_proposed",
            "avg_xsub_proposed",
            "avg_ratio_proposed",
        )
    )
    _gt = (
        GT(_gt_df, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(
            title=title,
            subtitle=subtitle,
        )
        .tab_spanner(
            label=label_spanner_default,
            columns=["avg_del_default", "avg_xsub_default"],
        )
        .tab_spanner(
            label=label_spanner_proposed,
            columns=[
                "delta_avg_delivery",
                "avg_del_proposed",
                "avg_cos_proposed",
                "avg_xsub_proposed",
                "avg_ratio_proposed",
            ],
        )
        .cols_label(
            avg_del_default="Avg. delivery bill",
            avg_xsub_default="Avg. cross-subsidy",
            delta_avg_delivery="Change in avg. delivery bill",
            avg_del_proposed="Avg. delivery bill",
            avg_cos_proposed="Avg. cost of service",
            avg_xsub_proposed="Avg. cross-subsidy",
            avg_ratio_proposed="Avg. cross-subsidy ÷ Avg. COS",
        )
        .tab_options(
            **get_switchbox_gt_tab_options(
                table_layout="auto",
                table_width="1280px",
            ),
        )
        .cols_width(
            subclass="132px",
            avg_del_default="128px",
            avg_xsub_default="128px",
            delta_avg_delivery="148px",
            avg_del_proposed="128px",
            avg_cos_proposed="124px",
            avg_xsub_proposed="118px",
            avg_ratio_proposed="112px",
        )
        .fmt_percent(columns=["avg_ratio_proposed"], decimals=1)
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=[
                "avg_del_default",
                "avg_xsub_default",
                "delta_avg_delivery",
                "avg_del_proposed",
                "avg_cos_proposed",
                "avg_xsub_proposed",
            ],
            decimals=0,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    return (
        _cos_gt_center_cells(
            _gt,
            data_columns=[
                "avg_del_default",
                "avg_xsub_default",
                "delta_avg_delivery",
                "avg_del_proposed",
                "avg_cos_proposed",
                "avg_xsub_proposed",
                "avg_ratio_proposed",
            ],
        )
    )


def run_scenario(path: str, label: str, *, hp_flat: bool) -> pl.DataFrame:
    _bat = load_master_bat(path)
    assert_bill_identity(_bat, label)
    validate_totals(_bat, label, hp_flat=hp_flat)
    return _bat


bat = run_scenario(PATH_MASTER_BAT_12, "run 1+2 (default)", hp_flat=False)

# Derive per-building annual kWh from the delivery bill and known tariff structure.
bat = bat.with_columns(
    ((pl.col("annual_bill_delivery") - _annual_fixed_per_customer) / _default_vol_usd_kwh)
    .alias("annual_kwh"),
)
_derived_total_kwh = float((bat["weight"] * bat["annual_kwh"]).sum())
assert abs(_derived_total_kwh - _ty_kwh) / _ty_kwh < 1e-6, (
    f"derived total kWh {_derived_total_kwh:,.0f} vs YAML {_ty_kwh:,.0f}"
)

cos_tbl = build_cos_table(bat)

# Aggregate kWh by subclass and join onto cos_tbl.
_kwh_by_ht = (
    bat.group_by("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2")
    .agg((pl.col("weight") * pl.col("annual_kwh")).sum().alias("total_kwh"))
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2").replace(HT_V2_LABELS).alias("subclass"),
    )
    .select("subclass", "total_kwh")
)
_total_kwh_agg = float(_kwh_by_ht["total_kwh"].sum())
_kwh_total_row = pl.DataFrame({"subclass": ["All customers"], "total_kwh": [_total_kwh_agg]})
_kwh_all = pl.concat([_kwh_by_ht, _kwh_total_row])
cos_tbl = (
    cos_tbl.join(_kwh_all, on="subclass", how="left")
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("total_kwh") / _total_kwh_agg).alias("pct_all_consumption"),
        (pl.col("total_kwh") / 1_000_000.0).alias("consumption_gwh"),
    )
)

report_vars["testimony_subclass_delivery_rows"] = cos_tbl.select(
    "subclass",
    "n_customers_display",
    "pct_customers_display",
    "consumption_gwh",
    "pct_all_consumption",
    "revenue_delivery",
    "pct_all_revenue",
).to_dicts()


def _cos_tbl_group_row(df: pl.DataFrame, subclass_label: str) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
    sub = df.filter(pl.col("subclass") == subclass_label)
    assert sub.height == 1, subclass_label
    return (
        float(sub["cost_of_service"][0]),
        float(sub["revenue_delivery"][0]),
        float(sub["cross_subsidy"][0]),
    )


_hp_cos, _hp_rev, _hp_xs = _cos_tbl_group_row(cos_tbl, "Heat pump")
_ng_cos, _ng_rev, _ng_xs = _cos_tbl_group_row(cos_tbl, "Natural gas")
_df_cos, _df_rev, _df_xs = _cos_tbl_group_row(cos_tbl, "Delivered fuels")
_er_cos, _er_rev, _er_xs = _cos_tbl_group_row(cos_tbl, "Electric resistance")

report_vars["cos_default_hp_group_cos"] = _hp_cos
report_vars["cos_default_hp_group_rev"] = _hp_rev
report_vars["cos_default_hp_group_xs"] = _hp_xs
report_vars["cos_default_ng_group_cos"] = _ng_cos
report_vars["cos_default_ng_group_rev"] = _ng_rev
report_vars["cos_default_ng_group_xs"] = _ng_xs
report_vars["cos_default_df_group_cos"] = _df_cos
report_vars["cos_default_df_group_rev"] = _df_rev
report_vars["cos_default_df_group_xs"] = _df_xs
report_vars["cos_default_er_group_cos"] = _er_cos
report_vars["cos_default_er_group_rev"] = _er_rev
report_vars["cos_default_er_group_xs"] = _er_xs

# Subsidy dollars received by natural gas + delivered-fuel subclasses (underpayment = negative BAT).
report_vars["cos_default_subsidy_to_natgas_and_delivered"] = -(_ng_xs + _df_xs)
report_vars["cos_default_ng_subsidy_received"] = max(0.0, -_ng_xs)
report_vars["cos_default_df_subsidy_received"] = max(0.0, -_df_xs)

# Aggregate % over/under cost of service (Σ revenue vs Σ COS for the subclass).
def _pct_rev_over_cos(rev: float, cos: float) -> float:
    return (rev - cos) / cos * 100.0 if cos else float("nan")


def _pct_rev_under_cos(cos: float, rev: float) -> float:
    return (cos - rev) / cos * 100.0 if cos else float("nan")


report_vars["cos_default_hp_pct_over_cos"] = _pct_rev_over_cos(_hp_rev, _hp_cos)
report_vars["cos_default_er_pct_over_cos"] = _pct_rev_over_cos(_er_rev, _er_cos)
report_vars["cos_default_ng_pct_under_cos"] = _pct_rev_under_cos(_ng_cos, _ng_rev)
report_vars["cos_default_df_pct_under_cos"] = _pct_rev_under_cos(_df_cos, _df_rev)
report_vars["cos_default_er_overpay_vs_cos"] = _er_rev - _er_cos


def find_fixed_charge_to_zero_hp_cross_subsidy(
    cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    current_customer_charge_monthly: float,
    total_revenue: float,
) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Find the customer charge that makes HP cross-subsidy = 0 under a uniform rate.

    Returns the new monthly customer charge, the increase, and the new volumetric rate.
    Assumes: only the customer charge and volumetric rate change; other fixed charges stay constant.
    """
    _all = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "All customers")
    _hp = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
    n_total = float(_all["n_customers"][0])
    n_hp = float(_hp["n_customers"][0])
    hp_cos = float(_hp["cost_of_service"][0])
    hp_kwh = float(_hp["total_kwh"][0])
    total_kwh = float(_all["total_kwh"][0])
    hp_xs = float(_hp["cross_subsidy"][0])

    # HP_rev_change = ΔF × 12 × (n_hp − n_total × hp_kwh / total_kwh)
    # Set HP_rev_change = −hp_xs (eliminate current overpayment)
    _kwh_share = hp_kwh / total_kwh
    _denom = 12.0 * (n_hp - n_total * _kwh_share)
    delta_f = -hp_xs / _denom
    new_customer_charge = current_customer_charge_monthly + delta_f
    new_vol_rate = (total_revenue - new_customer_charge * 12.0 * n_total
                    - (_annual_fixed_per_customer - current_customer_charge_monthly * 12.0) * n_total
                    ) / total_kwh
    return {
        "new_customer_charge_monthly": new_customer_charge,
        "customer_charge_increase_monthly": delta_f,
        "new_volumetric_delivery_rate": new_vol_rate,
        "current_volumetric_delivery_rate": _default_vol_usd_kwh,
        "current_customer_charge_monthly": current_customer_charge_monthly,
    }


_CURRENT_CUSTOMER_CHARGE_MONTHLY = 6.0
_fc_result = find_fixed_charge_to_zero_hp_cross_subsidy(
    cos_tbl,
    current_customer_charge_monthly=_CURRENT_CUSTOMER_CHARGE_MONTHLY,
    total_revenue=TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT,
)
for _k, _v in _fc_result.items():
    report_vars[f"fc_{_k}"] = _v

# Per-subclass data for the fixed-charge exhibit (CLF-JPV-6).
report_vars["fc_exhibit_rows"] = cos_tbl.select(
    "subclass",
    "n_customers",
    "consumption_gwh",
    "cost_of_service",
    "cross_subsidy",
).to_dicts()

_hp_row = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
_all_row = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "All customers")
report_vars["fc_hp_kwh"] = float(_hp_row["total_kwh"][0])
report_vars["fc_total_kwh"] = float(_all_row["total_kwh"][0])
report_vars["fc_hp_n_customers"] = float(_hp_row["n_customers"][0])
report_vars["fc_total_n_customers"] = float(_all_row["n_customers"][0])
report_vars["fc_hp_cos"] = float(_hp_row["cost_of_service"][0])
report_vars["fc_hp_cross_subsidy"] = float(_hp_row["cross_subsidy"][0])
report_vars["fc_other_fixed_annual_per_customer"] = (
    _annual_fixed_per_customer - _CURRENT_CUSTOMER_CHARGE_MONTHLY * 12.0
)
report_vars["fc_total_delivery_revenue"] = TOTAL_DELIVERY_REVENUE_REQUIREMENT
```

## Seasonal rate to eliminate HP cross-subsidy

The fixed-charge exhibit above asks: *what customer charge eliminates the HP cross-subsidy?* Here we ask the complementary question: *can seasonal volumetric rates eliminate the HP cross-subsidy while holding fixed charges constant?*

We define winter as October–March and summer as April–September, matching the seasonal HP tariff definition in `ri/config/periods/rie.yaml`. We load monthly electricity consumption from `load_curve_monthly` (upgrade=00, since run 1+2 is the status-quo stock), split each building's annual kWh into winter and summer, and then solve the 2×2 linear system:

1. **Revenue neutrality:** $r_w \cdot W_\text{all} + r_s \cdot S_\text{all} = R - F_\text{all}$
2. **HP cross-subsidy = 0:** $r_w \cdot W_\text{hp} + r_s \cdot S_\text{hp} = C_\text{hp} - F_\text{hp}$

where $r_w, r_s$ are winter/summer volumetric rates, $W, S$ are weighted seasonal kWh, $R$ is total delivery revenue, $F$ is total fixed delivery revenue, and $C_\text{hp}$ is HP cost-of-service.

```{python}
#| label: seasonal-rate-solve

from pathlib import Path as _Path

WINTER_MONTHS = [10, 11, 12, 1, 2, 3]
_ELEC_COL = "out.electricity.total.energy_consumption"
_LOAD_BASE = _Path("/ebs/data/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2_sb/load_curve_monthly/state=RI/upgrade=00")

_seasonal_kwh = (
    pl.scan_parquet(_LOAD_BASE)
    .select("bldg_id", "month", _ELEC_COL)
    .with_columns(pl.col("month").is_in(WINTER_MONTHS).alias("is_winter"))
    .group_by("bldg_id", "is_winter")
    .agg(pl.col(_ELEC_COL).sum().alias("season_kwh"))
    .collect()
)
_winter = _seasonal_kwh.filter(pl.col("is_winter")).select("bldg_id", pl.col("season_kwh").alias("winter_kwh"))
_summer = _seasonal_kwh.filter(~pl.col("is_winter")).select("bldg_id", pl.col("season_kwh").alias("summer_kwh"))
_bldg_seasonal = (
    _winter.join(_summer, on="bldg_id", how="outer_coalesce")
    .with_columns(pl.col("winter_kwh").fill_null(0.0), pl.col("summer_kwh").fill_null(0.0))
)

bat_seasonal = bat.with_columns(pl.col("bldg_id").cast(pl.Int64)).join(_bldg_seasonal, on="bldg_id", how="left")
_n_missing = bat_seasonal.filter(pl.col("winter_kwh").is_null()).height
assert _n_missing == 0, f"{_n_missing} buildings in BAT missing from monthly loads"

_annual_from_seasonal = bat_seasonal["winter_kwh"] + bat_seasonal["summer_kwh"]
```
